summary: Spolsky covers two topics: Fog Creek's solution to blog comment spam by routing URLs through a redirect server to prevent PageRank manipulation, and his impressions from the Microsoft PDC conference. He acknowledges that Microsoft's announced technologies like XAML and WinFS are genuinely impressive, but notes they are vaporware — years from shipping, with predecessors that did similar things long ago. He expresses skepticism that Microsoft will meet its stated timeline.
key_points: Blog comment spam exploits PageRank by having robots post URLs in discussion forums; Fog Creek countered this by routing links through a redirect server
Microsoft's PDC showcased XAML and WinFS as genuinely cool technologies, but none of it works yet and predecessors like NeWS and BeOS had similar capabilities years earlier
Microsoft's shipping timelines are unreliable — Spolsky predicts Longhorn will take at least three years, and likely longer
money_quotes: The PDC consisted mostly of what used to be called vaporware: preannouncements of cool products that are years and years away.
XAML is lightyears ahead of the old GDI/USER style of programming the user interface.
In WinFS a file is a database row, with arbitrary fields, and you can run efficient SELECT statements on files.
No, none of this is brand new.
Nobody will actually have Longhorn for about three years, if Microsoft keeps their schedule, which they won't.
key_insight: Impressive technology announcements mean little when the products are years from shipping and the ideas aren't even new — execution and delivery matter more than demos.
